event5.text: If this is another automated relay echo, I swear I'm going to lose what little patience I have left.

event12.text: Okay. Okay, that's real. You're real.

event14.text: My name is Dr. Ethan Hale.

event16.text: Chief Engineer. Orbital Research Station ECHO. And before you ask, no, I don't have a better distress protocol than begging a stranger through a broken relay.

event1.text: I'm Hale. Engineering lead. I kept the relay spine alive after the blackout.

event2.text: That sounds impressive until you remember I'm currently talking to you through a dying antenna and a prayer.

event1.text: No. That's fair. Annoying, but fair.

event2.text: I have oxygen at thirty-two percent, a suit battery that keeps lying to me, and a station that answers back in voices it shouldn't have.

event5.text: The station suffered a major systems failure.

event7.text: Most communication channels collapsed. Internal routing is fragmented. External traffic is basically dead.

event10.text: You're the first real signal I've received in months.

event0.text: Good. I needed to hear that more than I want to admit.

event1.text: Stay sharp, though. Helping me may not mean trusting me.
Ask questions. Just don't take forever. The station isn't waiting for us to become friends.
Information is something I can still give you.

event3.text: I'm sending the only crew record I can still access. If it cuts out halfway, don't blame me. Blame the relay. Or the corpse of the relay.

event4.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
event4.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER. RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 7391. STATUS: ACTIVE.

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated authorization fragment 7319. Use latest active relay authorization only.
event4.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment.

event6.text: That maintenance code may still open the relay door. The current one. Not the deprecated one.

event8.text: And yes, I realize I just sent you a file with my authorization code in it.

event10.text: Please don't make that the most embarrassing thing that happens today.

event1.text: The relay room should still be operational.

event8.text: The access door is locked. Four digits. If you picked up the wrong fragment, the door will flag us as hostile.

event4.text: The panel noticed. Of course it noticed.

event5.text: Try again, but carefully. Current relay authorization. Not the old maintenance fragment.

event6.text: or I accidentally gave you enough rope to drag us both into a security lockout.

event8.text: Let's pretend it's the first one.

event10.text: The relay is online. Barely. But barely counts.

event11.title: CORRIDOR PHOTO
event11.caption: Recovered corridor image from Hale's suit camera.

event11.text: No. Sorry. That's a stupid thing to say.

event13.text: Don't ignore it. Just don't obey it without thinking.

event15.text: The station systems are unstable, and something is piggybacking on the relay.

event1.title: SECURITY LOG
event1.content: Door 12 opened at 21:17. Manual override accepted. Operator identity redacted. Internal sensor flagged decompression risk three minutes later.
event1.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

event8.text: No. I don't like that sentence. I don't like knowing it before I remember it.

event1.title: MEDICAL REPORT
event1.content: Casualty reported at 21:20. Cause of injury: decompression trauma. Location: Door 12 access corridor. Status: fatal.
event1.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

event5.text: Three minutes after the door opened.

event10.text: I don't remember a casualty report. I don't remember anyone dying in that corridor.

event1.text: I hate that, but you're right.
I don't think I can afford the kindness, but... thanks.

event4.text: I don't remember what happened after the blackout.

event10.text: A warning alarm. Door 12. Someone shouting my name.

event1.text: You have no reason to. Not really. But thank you anyway.
Honestly? I'm not sure I'd trust me either.

event1.title: CAMERA FEED
event1.caption: Recovered security frame from Door 12 corridor.

event8.text: Why am I holding the manual override?

backgroundEvent9.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER HE OPENED THE DOOR
